GWA 4.0
Global Wind Atlas · Microscale ~250m · WAsP .lib · Free API

MERRA-2
NASA POWER API · Free · 1980→present

NCEP/CFSR
NOAA reanalysis · ~38km grid · 1979–2019, then CFSv2 · Independent of ERA5 — useful for cross-validation

NEWA
New European Wind Atlas · ~3km grid · Free · EU+surroundings

USA CONUS · 2007–2014 · Multi-height · Free API key · developer.nlr.gov

No data sources loaded
Terrain-Independent Steps: These steps require only wind data and mast parameters. No terrain or roughness data is needed.
① Vertical Extrapolation AUTO
Data height → Hub height via log-law + stability correction. Only requires roughness at mast location (a single value, not a map).
⚠ Power Law with α=1/7 is valid ONLY for flat, open terrain with neutral stability. It does NOT account for stability effects, roughness transitions, or complex terrain shear. Use Log-law + Stability for all other sites. Fit α from multi-height mast data when available.
—
Source WS m/s
—
Target WS m/s
—
Ratio / α
—
② MCP Long-term Correction
Correct short-term measurement to long-term climate using reference dataset. Only requires mast time-series and reanalysis data — no terrain needed.

—

MCP Summary Report
Directional Bias Assessment
Shows directional bias between target and reference before/after MCP. Significant bias (>15%) indicates the MCP may not adequately correct directional distribution.
Concurrent Period Representativeness
Compares concurrent period monthly means to long-term monthly means. If these diverge by more than 2%, the MCP may be unreliable.
③ Obstacles — Shelter Model
Nearby obstacles reduce wind speed. Applied before generalization. No terrain map required — only obstacle geometry.
⚠ Perera (1981) model is outdated and may not accurately represent complex obstacle environments. It assumes isolated rectangular obstacles and does not account for multiple wake interactions or turbulent recirculation zones properly.

Flat Terrain Fallback
If you don't have terrain data, you can skip terrain download and run analysis with flat terrain assumptions. This gives approximate results without orographic speed-ups.

—
Terrain Profile Along Wind Direction
Click on profile to set reference point · REF marker shows current reference
Roughness Map
Why roughness matters: Surface roughness (z₀) determines wind shear — taller obstacles = slower near-surface wind = higher shear. Different directions have different terrain (forest vs sea vs open fields), so per-sector roughness is needed for accurate WAsP-style OWC→GWC→SWC wind atlas chain.
Tries ESA CCI first, falls back to OSM Enhanced · Auto-generates roughness zones + 16-sector directional rose
Copernicus WFS — falls back to OSM Enhanced if endpoint is offline
Overpass API may timeout — use ESA CCI for reliability
Upload Land Cover CSV (lat,lon,code)CORINE/ESA codes auto-mapped to z₀

Roughness Rose (Auto-generated)
Per-sector z₀: Wind from the N may cross sea (z₀=0.0002) while wind from the S crosses forest (z₀=1.0). The rose captures this directional variation. Auto-populated from ESA CCI land cover.
Dirz₀¹x¹z₀²x²z₀³
Per-Sector IBL Height
Internal Boundary Layer height computed per sector from fetch distance and roughness change. Multi-step roughness changes flagged.

WAsP Analysis
One-button workflow: VE → MCP → Terrain → OW Flow → Wake → AEP — everything runs automatically, just like WAsP.
Load wind data first
▶ Advanced Options
Generalize (OWC → GWC)
WAsP standard = 0.03m
WAsP standard = 100m
1.0=WAsP default
Downscale (GWC → SWC)
GDL pathway: OWC→u*→Ug→u*_target→SWC. Note: GDL is applied analytically using geostrophic drag law with iterative solver. For complex terrain (RIX > 10%), flow model uncertainty increases to ±10-20% — use Bastankhah wake model and validate with mast data.
Ready
Generalization Results (OWC → GWC)
Removed mast orographic speed-up + roughness → GWC at standard height/roughness (via GDL pathway)

LowHigh
⚠ INDICATIVE ONLY — GWA/MESOSCALE-BASED ESTIMATE — NOT FOR INVESTMENT, PERMITTING, OR BANKABILITY USE
No validated mast dataset has been loaded. These results are derived from mesoscale reanalysis data (GWA/ERA5/MERRA2/NEWA) which does not resolve orographic speed-up, local roughness transitions, valley channelling, or ridge acceleration at individual turbine positions. GWA data is designed as a long-term reference for MCP, NOT as a primary site wind resource. AEP values shown are indicative screening estimates only. All bankable AEP results require: (1) on-site mast measurements with ≥12 months data, (2) validated terrain and roughness data, and (3) full OWC→GWC→SWC WAsP chain with directional MCP.

Uncertainty Analysis

Assumption Audit Log
Records all non-default assumptions and model settings for reproducibility and auditability.
No assumptions logged yet
Noise Assessment (ISO 9613-2 Simplified)
⚠ This is a simplified ISO 9613-2 broadband assessment. It does NOT replace a full assessment which requires: octave band source spectrum, topographic screening, multiple source paths, and reflection terms. For regulatory compliance, use dedicated noise assessment software such as WindPRO Noise, SoundPLAN, or CadnaA.
Air Density Details
Air density is computed using the hypsometric equation with ERA5T temperature and pressure when available, or ISA barometric as fallback. Per-turbine density correction accounts for elevation differences. Per IEC 61400-12-1 Annex F, power curve test results shall be density-corrected to site-specific air density using ρ_corr = P_site / (R_d × T_site), where the reference density for power curve normalization is 1.225 kg/m³.
—
Site ρ kg/m³
1.225
Ref ρ (IEC)

📑 Bankable EYA Report Inputs (IEC 61400-50-1 / FGW TR6 / MEASNET) — fill to generate bankable-grade report matching ProfEC Ventus structure

A. Report Metadata & Sign-off

B. Site Conditions

C. Equipment & Instrumentation Register

One row per sensor. Format: name,height_m,type,serial,calibration_source (comma-separated, one sensor per line)

D. Measurement Logbook

One row per incident. Format: start_date,end_date,comment

E. Long-term Correlation (MCP)

F. Sector-wise Wake Decay Constants (N.O. Jensen / Risø)

12 sectors. Format: sector,direction_range,wake_decay_constant

G. 5-Component Uncertainty Breakdown (FGW TR6)

ComponentUncertainty [%]Notes
1. Meteorological data
Measurement + vertical extrapolation + LT correction
2. Modeling of wind resource
Roughness + orographic + obstacle + flow model + T-RiX
3. Modeling of wake losses
Wake decay constant sensitivity test
4. Turbine data
Power curve + hysteresis + site-specific implementation
5. Energy losses
Icing, degradation, etc.
Overall uncertainty (RSS)—Auto-computed: √(u1² + u2² + u3² + u4² + u5²)

H. Site Compliance per Turbine (IEC 61400-1 ed.4)

One row per WTG. Format: id,Vref_ms,Vref_unc_ms,turbulence_class,shear_alpha,flow_incl_deg,terrain_complexity

I. Deviation Log

J. Standards & References (select all that apply)

K. Power Curve Type Certificate & Traceability

Bankable EYA requires the power curve to be traceable to a Type Certificate or site-specific power performance test (IEC 61400-12-1). Generic LUTs are not acceptable.

L. Calibration Certificate Register (file upload + SHA256 hash)

Upload each anemometer / vane calibration certificate (PDF). SHA256 hash is computed in-browser (file is NOT uploaded anywhere — stays local). The hash is embedded in the report Annex for traceability.
Cup anemometer calibrations (MEASNET)
Mast data file (CSV / WindPRO / WRA)
No file registered

M. Scope, Limitations & Independent Verification Attestation

⚠ Mandatory disclosure — single-developer tool, not commercially QA'd
WindFlow Pro is an open-source, single-developer tool. It is not a commercially-supported product like UL Openwind, EMD WAsP, or WindPRO. To produce a lender-acceptable bankable EYA, the following must be true:
  1. The mast dataset comes from a real measurement campaign per IEC 61400-50-1 (≥12 months, MEASNET-calibrated sensors).
  2. The site is non-complex (RIX < 15% in all sectors). For complex / escarpment sites, a CFD model (WindPRO/PROTEROS, ANSYS CFX, OpenFOAM) MUST be used instead of WindFlow Pro's linearized Jackson-Hunt flow.
  3. The Vref (extreme wind) analysis is performed with a real Gumbel PoT / MIS fit on long-term data (≥20 years) — WindFlow Pro can compute the fit from loaded mast data, but the result is only valid for the mast measurement period and MUST be cross-checked against a long-term reference.
  4. This report is independently verified by a third-party consultant (UL/DNV/UL/ProfEC Ventus/TÜV) before being submitted to lenders.

N. Compute Vref (Gumbel PoT) from Loaded Mast Data

When mast wind-speed time series is loaded (≥1 year of 10-min data), WindFlow Pro can fit a Gumbel distribution to Peak-over-Threshold storms and compute Vref for return periods 10/20/50/100 years. Results auto-populate the Section H per-turbine Vref column.
Produces a 14-section standalone HTML report matching the ProfEC Ventus structure. Gates: (1) attestation checkboxes in §M, (2) RIX < 15%, (3) mast data loaded or GWA-only disclosure.
Map Overview — Bankability Report
Site overview maps similar to DNV/UL bankability assessment reports. Each panel shows a key spatial dataset for the wind farm area.
